title: Day 21 Interview Prep
description: Week 3 capstone interview questions — Redux, RTK Query, auth, performance, architecture.

questions.question: Summarize Week 3 architecture in one app.
questions.sampleAnswer: Week 3 builds a medium app: Context for theme, Redux Toolkit for auth and client state, RTK Query for API cache and mutations, protected routes for auth, admin dashboard with role UI, and performance optimizations with memo and lazy routes on Day 20.

questions.question: Redux vs Context — how did you use both this week?
questions.sampleAnswer: Day 15 theme uses Context — infrequent updates, many readers. Day 16+ Redux for todos, auth, and RTK Query integration where DevTools, middleware, and predictable reducers matter. I do not put everything in Redux.

questions.question: Walk through RTK Query product delete updating the UI.
questions.sampleAnswer: User clicks delete. Component calls deleteProduct mutation. On success RTK Query invalidates Product tags. getProducts query refetches automatically. List re-renders with updated cache data. I show isLoading on button during mutation.

questions.question: Design auth + API together — where does token live?
questions.sampleAnswer: Token and user in auth slice. Login mutation or thunk sets both and persists token. prepareHeaders reads token for RTK Query. ProtectedRoute checks auth status after hydration. Logout clears slice and storage.

questions.question: How would you structure features folder for this app?
questions.sampleAnswer: features/auth, features/dashboard, features/products, features/theme each with components, hooks, types. app/ has store, router, providers. shared/ui for Button, Table, Badge. API slices in features/products/productsApi.ts.

questions.question: 150 minutes in — auth works but dashboard empty. Interviewer asks why. Respond.
questions.sampleAnswer: I prioritized auth and store setup in first 90 minutes per my strategy. Dashboard table is stubbed — RTK Query users endpoint wired but pagination incomplete. I would next add search filter and analytics derived from cached data. I communicate tradeoffs proactively in real interviews.

questions.question: Interviewer asks why you did not use React Query instead of RTK Query.
questions.sampleAnswer: This app already uses Redux Toolkit for auth and client state. RTK Query integrates in one store with one DevTools timeline. For greenfield without Redux I would consider TanStack Query. Choice is context-dependent not religious.

questions.question: Senior review: what would you refactor before production?
questions.sampleAnswer: Move JWT to httpOnly cookies with CSRF protection. Add global error boundary and 401 handler. Server-side pagination on dashboard. Tests for auth reducers and RTK Query endpoints with MSW. a11y on table and login form. Environment-based API URLs. Monitoring for Core Web Vitals.
redFlags: JWT in localStorage with no caveats
